sections.name: Sturzstromer
sections.text: Cobble mites that remain a cohesive swarm for many years become dangerous living landslides.

family.name: Cobble Mite

sections.name: Cobble Mite Pets
sections.text: Characters looking to keep a cobble mite as a pet can usually buy one in many large cities for 1 gp. Those who search for one in the wild must hunt carefully and risk finding enough cobble mites lairing together to form a cobbleswarm. A character with a familiar can choose a cobble mite, which must have the burrower familiar ability.

family.text: Cobble mites resemble squarish stones a few inches across with mouths like split geodes. Though indolent and harmless alone, they're dangerous in large groups.

traits.name: Uncommon
traits.text: Something of uncommon rarity requires special training or comes from a particular culture or part of the world. Some character choices give access to uncommon options, and the GM can choose to allow access for anyone. Less is known about uncommon creatures than common creatures. They typically can't be summoned. The DC of Recall Knowledge checks related to these creature is increased by 2.

traits.name: Aberration
traits.text: Aberrations are creatures from beyond the planes or corruptions of the natural order.

traits.name: Earth
traits.text: Effects with the earth trait either manipulate or conjure earth. Those that manipulate earth have no effect in an area without earth. Creatures with this trait consist primarily of earth or have a magical connection to that element. Planes with this trait are mostly solid, with caverns and other hollow pockets.

traits.name: Swarm
traits.text: A swarm is a mass or cloud of creatures that functions as one monster. Its size entry gives the size of the entire mass, though for most swarms the individual creatures that make up that mass are Tiny. A swarm can occupy the same space as other creatures, and must do so in order to use its damaging action. A swarm typically has weakness to effects that deal damage over an area (like area spells and splash weapons). Swarms are immune to the grappled, prone, and restrained conditions.

interaction_abilities.name: Clutching Stones
interaction_abilities.text: The sturzstromer's space is greater difficult terrain.

interaction_abilities.name: Tremorsense
interaction_abilities.text: A sturzstromer's tremorsense is a precise sense out to 120 feet and an imprecise sense out to 240 feet. A sturzstromer can't sense anything beyond the range of its tremorsense

universal_monster_ability.name: Tremorsense
universal_monster_ability.text: Tremorsense allows a monster to feel the vibrations through a solid surface caused by movement. It is an imprecise sense with a limited range (listed in the ability). Tremorsense functions only if the monster is on the same surface as the subject, and only if the subject is moving along (or burrowing through) the surface.

ability.critical_success: The creature falls prone, takes 1d6 bludgeoning damage, and is restrained by the sturzstromer until the end of the sturzstromer's next turn.
ability.name: Grasping Bites
ability.success: The creature falls prone and is grabbed by the sturzstromer until the end of the sturzstromer's next turn
ability.text: The sturzstromer attempts an Athletics check and compares the result to the Fortitude DC of each creature in its space. This counts as two attacks for the sturzstromer's multiple attack penalty.

traits.name: Attack
traits.text: An ability with this trait involves an attack. For each attack you make beyond the first on your turn, you take a multiple attack penalty.

ability.effect: The sturzstromer closes a fissure it made, choosing one creature that fell in. That creature must succeed at a DC 41 Reflex save or be buried as in an avalanche.
ability.name: Landslide
ability.requirement: The sturzstromer's most recent action was to cast *earthquake*

ability.name: Pummeling Assault
ability.text: Each foe in the sturzstromer's space takes 4d10 bludgeoning damage (DC 41 basic Reflex save).

universal_monster_ability.name: Tremorsense
universal_monster_ability.text: Tremorsense allows a monster to feel the vibrations through a solid surface caused by movement. It is an imprecise sense with a limited range (listed in the ability). Tremorsense functions only if the monster is on the same surface as the subject, and only if the subject is moving along (or burrowing through) the surface.
